# Fit a random-effects model

Now we fit the mixed-effects model: a population-level regression **plus** random
intercepts for each group (reef/site).

## The model

$$
y_i \sim \text{Normal}(\mu_i,\ \sigma), \qquad
\mu_i = \alpha + \beta\,x_i + b_{\text{group}[i]}, \qquad
b_j \sim \text{Normal}(0,\ \sigma_b)
$$

In FANGS:

```
model {
  for (i in 1:N) {
    y[i] ~ dnorm(mu[i], sigma)
    mu[i] <- alpha + beta * x[i] + b[group[i]]
  }
  for (j in 1:J) {
    b[j] ~ dnorm(0, sigma.b)
  }
  alpha   ~ dnorm(0, 50)
  beta    ~ dnorm(0, 10)
  sigma   ~ dunif(0, 50)
  sigma.b ~ dunif(0, 50)
}
```

- `J` — the number of groups. Set it in the constants alongside `N`.
- `group[i]` — which group observation `i` belongs to (FANGS encodes a text
  column for you).
- $\sigma_b$ — the **group-level SD**. It quantifies how much groups differ and
  controls the shrinkage.

## What to check

The **Summary** tab now includes $\sigma_b$ and each `b[j]`. Watch R-hat for
$\sigma_b$ especially — variance parameters are the slowest to mix.

## Your task

Fit the random-intercepts model to `random-effects.csv` and check $\alpha$,
$\beta$, and the group SD $\sigma_b$.
